theme.title: Rotation: Growth → Value & Defensives
theme.summary: Health Care (+2.63%) and Utilities (+2.21%) led the market today, while Technology (-2.71%) and Consumer Discretionary (-0.82%) brought up the rear. That split lines up with a rotation out of growth: growth-oriented sectors averaged -1.22% versus +2.29% for defensives and +1.14% for value and cyclicals, suggesting money moved from high-multiple names into steadier, cash-generative businesses. That shows up at the index level too: the blue-chip Dow gained +1.14% while the tech-heavy Nasdaq fell -0.80%, a wide enough gap to matter. Small caps underperformed as well, with the Russell 2000 at -0.55%, hinting the move favored large, established balance sheets over smaller, more rate-sensitive companies. The VIX fell 2.11% to 15.81, which reads as an orderly rotation rather than panic-driven selling. Gold added 1.49% to $4,187.30, crude oil was little changed at $68.78, and bitcoin slipped 0.08% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
